result.text:
Do not buy $SEI.

I’ve seen both community and ex-community members asking for a reason to buy $SEI.

To them, I’d say: Don’t buy SEI..

..unless you understand what @SeiNetwork  is actually building.

Don’t ask others whether you should buy—it’s your decision, not theirs.

I, or anyone else, can’t give financial advice.

But what I can do is share key facts, updates, and developments on SEI, so you can make an informed choice.

Only with proper information and right knowledge, you would be able to build conviction.

Let's start with some tech-talk.

1/ Sei Labs’ Speed Test: @jayendra_jog  tweeted about achieving 5 gigagas with a decentralized validator set in an internal devnet, making Sei the fastest Layer 1 blockchain.

SEI was able to achieve this way ahead of their scheduled target of March 31, 2025. Next, you can expect the launch of public devnet and then the SEI GIGA mainnet in the near future.

This speed is perfect for DeFi, trading, and real-time apps and is and will be crucial in driving massive adoption.

https://t.co/rLJKQ1sc3x

6/ Citrex Markets’ Liquidity Program: @citrex_markets
announced a Liquidity Enhancement Program on Twitter, offering a $550,000 prize pool to boost liquidity on Sei.

For those who do not know, Citrex is a self-custodial exchange on @SeiNetwork that offers CEX-like speed, deep liquidity, and low fees while ensuring DeFi transparency and security.

It features cross-margin collateral management for optimal capital efficiency, one-click trading for seamless execution, and leverage up to 20x (with 50x coming soon). The beta version is live, delivering a top-tier UX for traders.

https://t.co/uXq501kwBk

result.text:
GIGA isn’t just marketing. 

This level of speed is critical for DeFi, trading, and AI-driven applications.

Understand that:

A blockchain, or any project, doesn’t gain real users overnight. If it does, it fades just as fast.

While others were chasing hype with memecoins and daily rugs, SEI focused on infrastructure - building the foundation for next-gen dApps and consumer-facing products.

These were just some of the dapps I have mentioned. If I am not wrong, SEI has been working with close to 200 projects in their ecosystem.
